chapter: Chapter: Verghese Kurien—I Too Had A Dream

questions.question: Why is Verghese Kurien called the architect of the White Revolution?
questions.answer: Because he organized dairy cooperatives and transformed India’s milk production system.

questions.question: What vision guided Kurien’s work in the dairy sector?
questions.answer: He wanted to empower farmers through cooperative dairy development.

questions.question: Why was the cooperative model important for dairy farmers?
questions.answer: It helped them sell milk collectively and gain fair economic benefits.

questions.question: How did Kurien reduce the influence of middlemen in the dairy trade?
questions.answer: By establishing farmer-owned cooperatives.

questions.question: What was the main purpose of Operation Flood?
questions.answer: To expand milk production and improve milk distribution across India.

questions.question: What did the national milk grid achieve?
questions.answer: It connected rural milk producers with urban consumers.

questions.question: Why is Anand considered significant in Kurien’s story?
questions.answer: It became the headquarters of India’s cooperative dairy movement.

questions.question: What leadership quality helped Kurien achieve large-scale reforms?
questions.answer: Visionary leadership.

questions.question: How did Kurien’s initiatives support rural development?
questions.answer: They increased farmers’ income and strengthened rural economies.

questions.question: Why is AMUL considered a model cooperative?
questions.answer: It successfully combined farmer ownership with efficient marketing.

questions.question: What major change did the White Revolution bring to India?
questions.answer: India became one of the largest producers of milk in the world.

questions.question: How did Kurien combine technology and cooperation?
questions.answer: He introduced modern dairy technology within cooperative systems.

questions.question: Why were dairy cooperatives economically beneficial for farmers?
questions.answer: They ensured fair prices and stable income.

questions.question: What challenge did farmers face before Kurien’s reforms?
questions.answer: Low prices and exploitation by middlemen.

questions.question: What value does Kurien’s work highlight about cooperation?
questions.answer: Collective effort can improve economic and social conditions.

questions.question: Why is Kurien’s story an example of social entrepreneurship?
questions.answer: He used innovative ideas to solve social and economic problems.

questions.question: How did Kurien’s engineering background help the dairy sector?
questions.answer: It helped introduce efficient processing and production systems.

questions.question: What role did innovation play in the White Revolution?
questions.answer: It improved milk production, storage, and distribution.

questions.question: Why is Kurien’s leadership considered transformational?
questions.answer: Because it changed the structure of India’s dairy industry.

questions.question: How did Kurien’s work improve food security in India?
questions.answer: By increasing the availability of milk and dairy products.

questions.question: What leadership lesson can readers learn from Kurien?
questions.answer: A leader should work for the welfare of society.

questions.question: Why is Kurien’s story relevant today?
questions.answer: It shows how innovation and cooperation can solve social problems.

questions.question: What impact did Kurien’s reforms have on farmers?
questions.answer: They gained better income and economic independence.

questions.question: What does the title ‘I Too Had A Dream’ symbolize?
questions.answer: Kurien’s vision of transforming India’s dairy industry.

questions.question: What is the central message of the chapter?
questions.answer: Vision, cooperation, and determination can transform society.
